title: soilnircompiler: Calibration transfer between a low-cost NIR sensor and a research-grade spectroradiometer for soil property prediction
description: An R pipeline for spectral calibration transfer between a low-cost portable NIR sensor (NIRVascan NIR-S-G1, 900-1700 nm) and a research-grade ASD FieldSpec spectroradiometer (350-2500 nm). Six standardisation methods are benchmarked -- Direct Standardisation (DS), global and band-wise Correction Factor (CF), Piecewise Direct Standardisation (PDS), combined CF+DS, and a novel Regularised Band-wise Regression (RBR) -- to transfer low-cost sensor spectra into the reference instrument's spectral space. PLSR and Cubist models are then calibrated to predict soil total carbon, total nitrogen, pH (H2O), and plant-available phosphorus (Double Lactate extraction). Reference soil analyses were performed at the ZALF Central Laboratory (Muencheberg, Germany).

creators.name: de Sousa Mendes, Wanderson
creators.affiliation: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO)
